title: [CP7] Admin panel: ZIP export and import
description:
Mirrors GitHub #23. Admin-only backup/restore feature for CP7. Lets an admin download a ZIP containing the SQLite database + covers directory, and upload a backup ZIP to restore state.

Design captured in DEC-027 (DECISIONS.md). Summary of the seven decisions:

1. ZIP scope: data/database.sqlite + data/covers/ only. Sessions table preserved on import. Logs/config/binary/templates/static/.beads excluded.

2. Export consistency: SQLite VACUUM INTO '/tmp/snapshot.sqlite' for a consistent point-in-time copy before zipping. Rules out torn-write corruption under concurrent writes.

3. Import strategy: in-process swap with global sync.RWMutex. Import handler takes write lock, closes DB, swaps files, reopens. Other handlers take read lock for DB access. No restart required.

4. Pre-swap backup: rename existing files to .bak before overwrite. Rename back on reopen failure. Keep most recent .bak indefinitely. Exclude *.bak from export walk so backups don't recursively contain previous backups.

5. Zip Slip protection: new internal/safezip/ package (first internal/ in this repo). SafeExtract validates every entry via filepath.Rel boundary check; rejects symlinks. Reusable for deferred #63 offline cover sync.

6. No CLI counterpart in CP7: web UI export only. Defer the CLI subcommand to whenever #63 is picked up post-submission.

7. Import confirmation: medium friction. Bootstrap modal + 'I have a backup' checkbox; Confirm button disabled until ticked.

Implementation plan (3 sessions):

Session 1: internal/safezip/ package + tests; export handler with VACUUM INTO; admin template stats panel scaffold.
Session 2: import handler with swap + lock + rollback; admin template import form with checkbox interlock; .bak handling.
Session 3: integration smoke + boundary tests (Zip Slip rejection mandatory per #62 acceptance); branch merge prep.

Acceptance:
- Export downloads a valid ZIP containing DB + covers, generated from VACUUM INTO snapshot.
- Import accepts a valid backup ZIP, swaps in-process under write lock, leaves a recoverable .bak.
- Import rejects: ZIPs with path-traversal entries, ZIPs with absolute-path entries, ZIPs with symlinks.
- Admin stats panel shows book/patron/active-loans/overdue/total-loans counts.
- Confirmation modal blocks accidental clicks.

Branch: cp7-admin-backup. Merges via PR (per feature-branch-pr-required memory).
notes:
Session 1 paused 2026-04-26 mid-design for internal/safezip package. Resume here next session.

PROPOSED PACKAGE SHAPE (Tim has not yet approved or pushed back):

Files: internal/safezip/extract.go + extract_test.go (+ optional doc.go).

Public API:
- Sentinel errors: ErrZipSlip, ErrSymlink, ErrAbsolutePath, ErrTooLarge.
- Limits struct {MaxFileSize int64, MaxTotalSize int64}; 0 = unlimited per field.
- DefaultLimits = Limits{100<<20, 500<<20} (100MB per file, 500MB total).
- SafeExtract(zipPath, destDir string) error -- uses DefaultLimits.
- SafeExtractWithLimits(zipPath, destDir string, limits Limits) error.
- SafePath(destDir, entryName string) (string, error) -- exposes the boundary check standalone for #63's manifest-peek-before-extract case.

KEY DESIGN CHOICE: two-pass extraction. Pass 1 walks every entry and validates (path, mode, size). Pass 2 extracts. This makes extraction atomic at the archive level -- either all entries land or none do, never partial state from a malicious entry mid-archive.

Errors wrapped with fmt.Errorf so callers do errors.Is(err, ErrZipSlip).

OPEN DECISION POINTS (4 -- Tim to settle next session):
1. Three exported functions or drop SafePath until #63 forces it.
2. Default limit values: 100MB/500MB OK or tighter?
3. Test coverage: 18 cases drafted (happy path, zip slip variants, absolute paths, symlink, size caps, two-pass atomicity, SafePath direct usage, edge cases like empty names / NUL bytes / dot-only names). Cut any?
4. doc.go for package godoc: yes/no.

TEST CASES PLANNED (full list in conversation transcript, summary):
- Happy: simple ZIP, empty ZIP, nested-but-safe path.
- Zip Slip: ../etc/passwd, subdir/../../etc/passwd, Windows-style separators.
- Absolute: /etc/passwd, C:\Windows.
- Symlink: ZIP entry with os.ModeSymlink set.
- Size: per-file cap, total cap, zero=unlimited.
- Atomicity: bad entry at index 5 of 10 leaves zero files on disk.
- SafePath: direct calls for happy path + traversal.
- Edge cases: empty name, '.' name, NUL byte in name.

NEXT SESSION: Tim picks A/B/C/D on the four decision points; we lock the API; then I show extract.go in tutor mode (this file is package-internal Go, written by Tim, not the test exception). Tests are exception territory and I can write extract_test.go directly per CLAUDE.md exception list.

Branch: cp7-admin-backup. DEC-027 captures the parent CP7 #23 design.

title: [CP7] Test coverage push (final session)
description:
Dedicated coverage session at the end of CP7, after #23 (admin panel ZIP export/import) and #24 (security hardening + deploy) have landed.

Why last: most of CP7 ships new code (admin handlers, security middleware, rate limiting, cache-busting). Doing the coverage push last means we cover that new code in the same session it gets reviewed for the security-review pass, rather than topping up coverage twice.

Baseline (2026-04-25): 61.8% total. Already-cleaned: LoadUser deleted as dead code (cs408-go-stack-3oj).

Scope:
- Open the HTML coverage view (go tool cover -html=coverage.out) and walk every red region in security-sensitive code: handlers_auth.go, handlers_*.go middleware-protected branches, db.go transactional methods (CheckoutBook, ReturnBook, UpdateUserPassword, CreatePatron, DeletePatron), validators.go.
- Fill the 0% Open Library trio: parsePublishYear (pure func, table test), normalizeOpenLibraryBook (feed canned response), FetchOpenLibraryBook (httptest.NewServer with stubbed JSON, plus error-path tests for 4xx/5xx/malformed).
- Fill 0% on covers.go:SaveCoverFromURL (httptest.NewServer returning real PNG) and the new admin export/import handlers (Zip Slip rejection test required).
- Add HandleBookNew test (currently 0%, GET /books/new with staff session asserts 200 + form fields).
- Boost partial-coverage handlers (HandleCatalog 50%, HandleMyLoans 50%, HandlePatronList 50%) -- the missing branches are typically empty-state and error paths.
- Skip (low signal): main.main, FetchAndStoreSeedCovers, extensionForImageMime.

Targets:
- Security-sensitive layers (auth, middleware, validators, transactional DB methods): 90%+
- Handlers: 80%+
- Overall: 75%+ (up from 61.8% baseline; admin panel and security middleware code adds new lines so the denominator grows during CP7)

Acceptance:
- go test ./... -cover prints >= 75% total
- go tool cover -func=coverage.out shows no 0% in handlers_auth.go, validators.go, openlibrary.go, covers.go (skipped exceptions documented in close notes)
- Zip Slip rejection test exists for the admin import path
- govulncheck output captured in close notes (paired with the supply-chain check from #24)

Sequencing: do NOT start until #23 and #24 have shipped their feature work. The 'finish CP7' session is the natural close-out point alongside the integration smoke.

title: CP7: LoadUser middleware shows 0% coverage -- investigate and fill gap
description: LoadUser is the middleware that hydrates the current user struct from the session cookie. Coverage report (61.4% total) shows it at 0.0%. Either (a) loginAs test helper bypasses LoadUser by setting c.Set('user', ...) directly so handler tests never exercise the real auth path, or (b) LoadUser is not actually wired into the middleware chain (would be a real bug). Investigate which, then either add direct unit tests for LoadUser or refactor loginAs to drive it through the cookie path. This is CP7 hardening scope -- the auth middleware is exactly the layer that needs real coverage for the security review.
notes: Investigation found LoadUser was never registered in main.go or setupTestRouter -- the 0% coverage was correctly flagging dead code, not a missing test. Deleted handlers_auth.go:122-139. grep confirmed zero callers before delete. Build clean, full test suite passes, total coverage 61.4% -> 61.8%.
close_reason: Dead code removed (was never wired in any middleware chain)

title: CP6 session 7: integration smoke + role-boundary pass + close
description: Final CP6 close-out: full live integration smoke (admin login -> add book -> checkout to patron -> patron dashboard + /my/loans + return), role-boundary recheck, govulncheck/go vet pass, docs sync (CLAUDE.md state line + checkbox updates), local merge cp6-loans -> main. EC2 redeploy is a manual step Tim runs; this session prepares the merge commit and the runbook.

title: CP6 session 5: kiosk public browse (/kiosk + /kiosk/books/:id)
description: Public anonymous catalog and read-only book detail under a kiosk-only layout shell (no sidebar, no nav). Reuses the catalog grid and book detail data minus staff/admin controls (no Add/Edit/Delete, no checkout form, no patron list, no loan history). New /kiosk/books/:id route so anon clicks on a kiosk card don't bounce through /login. New kiosk_layout.html shared by both kiosk pages.

title: CP6 session 4: role-differentiated dashboard cards + /my/loans
description: Render essential dashboard cards per role (staff/admin: Overdue, Active Loans, Out of Stock; patron: My Active Loans with next due date) and add /my/loans patron-only route. Tighten CountActiveLoans to exclude overdue so its count matches /loans?filter=active.

title: CP6 session 3b: loan handlers + routes + HandleBookDetail patron fetch
description: Session 3b: wire the checkout form to a real handler end-to-end. Write HandleCheckout, HandleReturn, HandleLoansList in a new handlers_loans.go. Update HandleBookDetail to fetch patrons for the checkout dropdown. Register routes in main.go. Handler tests land as a follow-up issue once handlers exist.
acceptance_criteria:
- HandleBookDetail fetches and passes patrons to the template
- handlers_loans.go has HandleCheckout, HandleReturn, HandleLoansList
- All three routes registered in the staff+admin route group
- 'loans' added to templateNames in main.go
- Flash banner shows correct text on each success/guard path
- go build ./... passes
- end-to-end checkout + return flow works in the browser
close_reason: 3 handlers + routes + HandleBookDetail patron fetch + 13 handler tests; full suite passes

title: CP6 session 3a: Claude-completable frontend scaffolding
description: Complete the parts of session 3 that don't depend on Tim's handler implementation: flash codes (data entry), /loans template (new), and book-detail checkout scaffold wire-up. Handler tests deferred to a follow-up issue once handlers exist.
acceptance_criteria:
- 6 loan flash codes added to flash.go
- /loans template renders active and overdue filters
- book-detail checkout scaffold posts to /books/:id/checkout
- go build ./... passes
close_reason: 6 flash codes + book-detail wire-up + /loans template. Build + all tests green.

title: CP6 session 2 follow-up: loan DB method tests
description: Add test coverage for the 8 loan DB methods landed in commit a3cc917. Required per AGENTS.md Testing Requirements; missed during initial session 2 close-out. Covers: CheckoutBook (3 guards + tx happy path), ReturnBook (guard + tx happy path), GetActiveLoans / GetOverdueLoans / GetPatronActiveLoans (filter correctness + days_overdue computation + ORDER BY), CountActiveLoans / CountOverdueLoans / CountOutOfStock (row-count assertions).
acceptance_criteria:
- Unit tests exist for all 8 loan DB methods
- Happy path covered for each
- CheckoutBook guards tested at boundary (at limit, one over limit, one overdue)
- ReturnBook idempotency guard tested
- go test ./... passes
close_reason: 13 loan DB method tests in db_loans_test.go; all pass under go test ./...

title: CP6 session 2: remaining loan DB methods
description: Complete the loan backend DB layer on cp6-loans branch. CheckoutBook and ReturnBook are done. Remaining: GetActiveLoans, GetOverdueLoans, GetPatronActiveLoans (all return []LoanListRow), plus CountActiveLoans, CountOverdueLoans, CountOutOfStock for dashboard cards. All methods support DEC-024 loan state model and DEC-026 guardrails.
close_reason: 8 loan DB methods + schema rewrite + sentinels + constants + LoanListRow struct; DECs 024-026 written

title: Login page: link to kiosk for anonymous browse
description: Small UX polish: login page now advertises /kiosk via a 'Browse the catalog without an account' link below the 'Library Portal' subtitle. Helps a visitor who lands on /login without credentials discover the public catalog instead of bouncing.
notes: One-line addition to templates/login.html: Browse the catalog without an account under the Library Portal subtitle. Hardcoded href so no XSS surface; same-origin so no rel=noopener concern; /kiosk was already public so no new info exposure. go vet clean, full test suite (169 tests) passes.

title: Out-of-Stock catalog filter so dashboard card can link
description: The Out-of-Stock dashboard card on staff/admin home is currently non-clickable because /catalog has no server-side or client-side filter for quantity_available=0. Add an ?out=1 (or similar) query param + matching client-side toggle in app.js so the card can link to /catalog?out=1 and show only books that are completely checked out. Pairs with the broader catalog pagination work (Path 2 AJAX fragment swap) deferred from CP6 -- if that ships first, fold this filter in there.

title: [Backlog] Bulk cover upload + needs-cover report
description:
Two-part librarian-workflow feature for the long tail of books that no automated cover provider finds. Part A is a 'books needing covers' report so a librarian can see exactly which books are missing covers. Part B is a bulk-upload UI that accepts a folder of files named by ISBN and matches them to books in one operation.

Why: even with Google Books (cs408-go-stack-2g0) layered on top of Open Library, a meaningful fraction of books will have no automated cover available -- old editions, regional publishers, niche academic press. Today the only way to add covers for those is one at a time through the edit form, which is impractical for a stack of 50 books a librarian wants to handle in one session. This feature targets that workflow specifically: scan a stack with a phone camera, name files by ISBN, drag the folder onto the page, done.

Design when un-deferred:

Part A: Needs-cover report.

Two viable shapes:
1. Filter on the existing /catalog page: ?needs_cover=1. Cheap to add; reuses existing render. Cons: catalog has to switch between staff/admin/patron views; the filter is mostly useful to staff/admin only.
2. Dedicated /admin/needs-covers page: clean separation; admin-only; can include staff workflow tools (per-row 'Upload cover' button, 'Mark as no-cover-available' option for books that legitimately have no cover available anywhere).

Recommendation: Path 2 (dedicated admin page). Cleaner separation, room to grow, doesn't pollute the patron-facing catalog filter set.

DB query: SELECT id, isbn, title, authors_concat FROM books WHERE cover_filename IS NULL OR cover_filename = '' ORDER BY title. Reuses existing book row shape.

Per-row: ISBN (formatted), title, primary author, 'Upload cover' button. Click opens a modal scoped to cover-only upload (reuses the existing covers.go validation path).

Part B: Bulk upload UI.

Drag-drop area on /admin/needs-covers (or its own /admin/bulk-cover-upload route -- prefer same page so the report and the action are co-located).

Form accepts multiple files via <input type='file' multiple accept='image/*'>. Each file's filename is parsed for an ISBN: matches /^(\d{10}|\d{13})\.(jpg|jpeg|png|webp)$/i. ISBN normalization: strip hyphens, validate ISBN-10/ISBN-13 checksum, allow either form to match a books.isbn row (which is stored in canonical form -- need to check schema for which form).

Server flow:
1. Parse uploaded files; reject any with no ISBN-shaped filename.
2. For each valid file: validate via existing covers.go magic-byte path (size, MIME, extension).
3. Look up the book by ISBN. If no book matches, that file goes in the 'no-match' bucket.
4. If book exists: write cover to DATA_DIR/covers/<existing-naming-pattern>, UPDATE books SET cover_filename = ? WHERE id = ?. Old cover (if any) deleted from disk after the DB write succeeds (matches the existing UpdateBookCover pattern).
5. Render result page: applied N (with thumbnails of each), skipped M (no ISBN match -- list filenames), validation_failed K (list filenames + reason).

UX details:
- Confirmation modal before applying any changes (the bulk upload could overwrite existing covers if a book happens to already have one). Modal lists what will happen.
- Drag-drop also accepts a folder via webkitdirectory attribute on the input; works across modern browsers.
- Per-file size cap (e.g. 5MB) and total upload cap (e.g. 200MB) to prevent UI hangs and OOM on the server.

Tests:
- DB query unit test (books with empty/null/non-empty cover_filename are categorized correctly).
- Handler test: happy path with 3 files, 2 matching, 1 no-match. Confirms applied count + skipped count + cover_filename DB updates.
- Boundary tests: file with non-ISBN filename, file failing magic-byte check, file matching ISBN that does not exist in books, file too large.
- Integration: admin uploads, verifies catalog page shows the new covers afterward.

Sequencing. Independent of cs408-go-stack-o03 (placeholder) and cs408-go-stack-2g0 (Google Books). Recommended ship order: o03 first, 2g0 second, this third. The placeholder + Google Books reduce the rate at which manual upload is needed; this issue makes manual upload efficient when it is needed.

Cross-refs:
- cs408-go-stack-o03 / #65: placeholder is the visual fallback for books still needing covers.
- cs408-go-stack-2g0 / #66: Google Books reduces the books-needing-covers rate.
- Touches: handlers_books.go (or new handlers_admin_covers.go), new admin template, app.js (drag-drop UI), covers.go (validation reused, possibly small refactor to expose batch-validation entry point), book DB methods (small new query).

title: [Backlog] Google Books fallback cover provider
description:
Add Google Books as a tier-2 cover source after Open Library fails. Public Google Books API: https://www.googleapis.com/books/v1/volumes?q=isbn:<isbn>. Returns JSON containing imageLinks.thumbnail (Google-hosted, curated, no Amazon ToS issues).

Why: today, when an OL ISBN lookup returns no cover, the book is permanently coverless until a librarian uploads one manually. Google Books has its own cover database with significant overlap and significant disjoint coverage (each source has books the other lacks). Adding a second source meaningfully reduces the rate at which books fall through to the placeholder fallback.

Design when un-deferred:

API key. Free tier covers small libraries (~1000 requests/day). Beyond that, requires a Google Cloud API key. Make the key optional via env var GOOGLE_BOOKS_API_KEY; if unset, the provider attempts unauthenticated requests (works for low volume) and degrades gracefully to skipping the source if the rate-limit cap is hit.

Provider interface refactor. This issue is the trigger to introduce the CoverProvider interface:

  type CoverProvider interface {
      Name() string
      FetchByISBN(ctx context.Context, isbn string) (CoverResult, error)
  }

  type CoverResult struct {
      ImageURL string
      Source   string // 'openlibrary' | 'googlebooks'
  }

  type Cascade struct {
      providers []CoverProvider
  }
  func (c *Cascade) Fetch(ctx, isbn) (CoverResult, error)

Cascade.Fetch tries each provider in order; first success wins. On all-providers-failed, the caller falls back to the placeholder generator (cs408-go-stack-o03).

Where the code lives. New internal/cover/ package (paired with the internal/openlibrary/ refactor flagged in #63's design notes). Move openlibrary.go into internal/cover/openlibrary.go; add internal/cover/googlebooks.go; cascade lives in internal/cover/cascade.go. The handler layer changes from calling openlibrary.FetchByISBN directly to calling cover.DefaultCascade.Fetch, which is a small refactor in handlers_books.go.

Refactor scope. internal/cover/ houses: openlibrary.go (existing OL client moved + maybe split into provider implementation), googlebooks.go (new), cascade.go (new), and shared types (CoverProvider, CoverResult). The 'cover image upload + magic-byte validation + DATA_DIR/covers/ storage' code in covers.go stays in package main -- it's about HTTP upload handling and disk storage, not about fetching from external sources.

Tests. httptest.NewServer mocking the Google Books JSON response. Cases: happy path, no-cover-in-response, malformed JSON, 4xx, 5xx, network error. Cascade tests: OL hit, OL miss + Google hit, both miss, both error.

Sequencing. Independent of cs408-go-stack-o03 (placeholder) and the bulk-upload issue. Recommended order to ship is placeholder first (gives never-empty UI baseline) then this (reduces placeholder rate). Internal/cover/ refactor lands as part of this issue or as a precursor PR if scope allows splitting.

Cross-refs:
- cs408-go-stack-o03: placeholder is the catch-all when both providers miss.
- #63 (cs408-go-stack-0eh): the internal/openlibrary/ refactor flagged there is subsumed by this issue's broader internal/cover/ refactor. When #63 is picked up, its connected fetcher binary imports internal/cover/ and gets the cascade for free.
- Touches: openlibrary.go (moved), handlers_books.go, new internal/cover/ tree, possibly cover-fetching call sites in seed paths.

title: [Backlog] Placeholder cover generator (last-resort fallback)
description:
When no cover provider returns a cover for a book, render a deterministic SVG placeholder so the catalog UI never has a blank slot. SVG contains the title + author on a colored background; background color is hashed from the title so the same book always gets the same placeholder color (visual consistency across renders).

Why: today, books without a cover render a blank slot in the catalog and book-detail pages. Looks unfinished and makes scanning the catalog harder. A deterministic placeholder is better than nothing and is the natural last layer of any cover-fetching cascade. Foundational for cover-fallback work because it guarantees every book has SOME cover, even if no automated provider succeeds.

Design when un-deferred:

Generation. Pure function: PlaceholderSVG(title, author string) []byte. Layout: title in larger bold text centered upper-half, author smaller below. Font sizes auto-scale to fit (long titles get smaller font). Background: deterministic color from a curated palette of ~12 muted/readable colors, picked via crc32(title) % len(palette). Foreground: white text or near-white for contrast.

Storage. On-demand, not cached to disk. SVG generation is fast (microseconds), output is tiny (~1KB), and on-demand means the algorithm can change without invalidating cached files. The catalog template references a stable URL like /covers/placeholder/<book-id>.svg which a handler resolves to the generated bytes.

Handler. New GET /covers/placeholder/:id route in the public group (no auth needed -- the kiosk needs placeholders too). Looks up the book by id, calls PlaceholderSVG(book.Title, normalizeAuthors(book.Authors)), returns image/svg+xml.

Template integration. The catalog and book-detail templates currently reference book.cover_filename to build the cover URL. Add a template helper or server-side computation: if cover_filename is empty, point the cover img src at /covers/placeholder/<id>.svg instead of /covers/<filename>. Both kiosk and authenticated catalog get the same treatment.

Where the code lives. Future internal/cover/ package (post-#63 refactor) is the natural home for placeholder.go. For CP7-era code, putting it in covers.go (existing file, package main) is fine. The refactor to internal/cover/ ships when #63 is picked up.

Tests. Unit test for PlaceholderSVG: same input always produces same output (determinism), output is valid SVG (xml-parseable), output contains the title text. Handler test: known book id returns 200 + image/svg+xml content-type + non-empty body. Boundary: bad id returns 404.

Sequencing relative to siblings. This issue is independent of the Google Books provider and the bulk-upload feature. File order in the backlog group is B then A then C purely on marginal-value layering: B gives never-empty UI; A reduces placeholder rate; C handles the books neither A nor OL ever finds.

Cross-refs:
- Adjacent: Google Books fallback provider (placeholder is the catch-all when both OL and Google Books miss).
- Adjacent: Bulk cover upload + needs-cover report.
- Touches: covers.go, catalog.html, kiosk.html, book_detail.html, kiosk_book_detail.html.

title: [Backlog] Server-side catalog pagination -- Path 2 (AJAX fragment swap)
description:
Mirrors GitHub #37 (which originally captured a v2 server-side-with-form-submit design that was downgraded UX). Path 2 is the right approach: AJAX fragment swap preserves the live-filter feel of the current client-side render while gaining true server-side pagination at scale.

Why deferred: the current all-in-DOM catalog render at 100 seeded books filters instantly via client-side JS and the UX is genuinely good. Server-side with full-page form submit (v2 shape) was a UX downgrade. Unblocked when catalog routinely exceeds ~500 rows or when post-submission session frees up.

Path 2 design:

Templates: extract catalog grid + pagination controls into a named {{define "catalog_results"}} template block.

Handler: HandleCatalog branches on X-Requested-With: XMLHttpRequest (or ?partial=1) to render just that block on AJAX requests, full layout on direct loads.

Frontend (app.js): debounces the search input (~300ms), genre/available change events fire immediately, each interaction calls fetch() with the AJAX header, swaps #catalog-results innerHTML, updates URL via history.replaceState (search keystrokes) or pushState (discrete state changes), uses AbortController to prevent stale-wins races.

No-JS fallback: full-page form submit. Same handler renders the full template when not asked for the partial.

Fragment pattern reuses for /loans filtering if that page ever outgrows all-in-DOM.

DB layer when built:
- ListBooks(filter, page, pageSize) (BookPage, error)
- GetAllGenres() ([]string, error)
- count query sharing the same WHERE clause
- Author search via correlated EXISTS subquery to avoid fighting the GROUP_CONCAT aggregate currently used in the catalog query

Pagination widget: numbered pages with ellipsis, 24 per page.

Full design preserved in docs/cp6-planning.md under 'Catalog Pagination (Path 2: AJAX fragment swap)'.

Mirrors GitHub #37.

title: [Backlog] Trim CLAUDE.md after CP7 close
description: CLAUDE.md is currently 279 lines / 38KB / ~5,400 words and gets loaded into every Claude conversation turn -- non-trivial context cost. Bloat sources: per-CP retrospective summaries duplicate what is already in git log + DECISIONS.md + bd memories; deferred backlog entries duplicate what is already in bd issues + GitHub issues + project board. Going forward (this project + future projects), CLAUDE.md should hold standing context only: working agreements, hard rules, stack, commands, gotchas, pointers. Tasks and design notes live in bd. Concrete trim plan when un-deferred (post-2026-05-01 submission): (1) collapse 'Current State' CP1-CP6 paragraphs to one-liners pointing at bd memories + git log; (2) replace deferred-backlog design dumps with one-line bd-id pointers (e.g. '- Offline cover sync (cs408-go-stack-0eh)'); (3) keep working agreements, hard rules, stack/commands cheatsheet, gotchas, key references untouched. Target: under 100 lines, under 12KB. Do NOT do this before the 5/1 submission -- the current detail is useful for the deadline crunch and trimming risks losing context that has not yet been mirrored elsewhere. Confirm bd issues / memories cover all the detail before deleting from CLAUDE.md.
notes: Done in same session as filing. Two-pass approach: (1) audited bd coverage of CLAUDE.md content, found 4 sync gaps -- created cs408-go-stack-3d0 (Path 2 pagination, no bd issue existed), updated cs408-go-stack-eig (loan history) and cs408-go-stack-zcs (OL offline detection) with full design depth, created bd memory cp5-architecture; (2) trimmed CLAUDE.md from 279 lines / 38KB to 258 lines / 12KB. Character count -68%, the actual context-cost win. Sections now: standing context (kept verbatim), Current State (collapsed to bd-memory pointers), CP7 active scope (kept), backlog (one-liner per item with bd id pointer).
close_reason: Done in same session, before CP7 close per user direction

title: [Backlog] Offline cover sync via flash drive (3-machine workflow)
description: Air-gap workflow for fetching Open Library covers when the LibreShelf server has no internet access. Designed for the same restricted-network persona as cs408-go-stack-o1x (self-reg toggle off): prison libraries, secure facilities, anywhere policy or topology blocks outbound HTTP from the production box. Today, a book row created without a cover stays coverless forever on an offline install -- the only way to add covers is to manually upload one per book through the edit form. With many books, that is impractical. This feature creates a courier workflow where an admin uses a personal laptop with internet to ferry covers to the offline server via flash drive. Three machines, three artifacts: (1) offline server runs an export utility that scans the books table for rows missing a cover and writes a manifest JSON; admin copies to flash drive. (2) Connected machine runs a fetcher tool that reads the manifest, queries OL for each book, validates downloads via the existing covers.go magic-byte path, packages into a return ZIP. (3) Offline server admin uploads the ZIP, server validates Zip Slip + server_id match + per-cover MIME, writes files to DATA_DIR/covers/, updates books.cover_filename. Sequencing: depends on settings table from cs408-go-stack-o1x (for server_id), Zip Slip helper from #23 (CP7), and a refactor of openlibrary.go and covers.go into reusable internal/ packages so the connected fetcher binary can import them without dragging in the web app. Cross-refs cs408-go-stack-zcs (offline detection on lookup button -- adjacent connectivity feature) and #23 (admin ZIP export/import -- reuses Zip Slip helper).

title: [Backlog] Patron self-registration toggle (admin-controlled)
description:
Admin-only setting on /admin that gates patron self-registration. Default OFF: only staff and admin can create patron accounts (current CP5 behavior, suitable for restricted environments like prisons or correctional facilities where inmates must not self-register). When toggled ON: a public /register route is exposed that lets a patron create their own account.

Why this matters: LibreShelf's design assumes a small library context, but the deployment environment changes the trust model. A public library wants the friction of self-service signup; a prison library cannot allow it. Making this a runtime toggle (not a build flag) lets the same binary serve both deployments.

Design questions when un-deferred:

Settings storage. Add a settings table (key TEXT PRIMARY KEY, value TEXT, updated_at TEXT, updated_by INTEGER FK users.id) rather than a one-row config blob, so future toggles slot in cleanly. Cache in memory at startup with a write-through invalidate on update. Default-deny: missing key reads as OFF.

Admin UI. New section on /admin (admin-only, already gated). Bootstrap toggle switch with a confirmation modal on flip (Are you sure you want to allow patrons to create their own accounts?). Server records updated_by + updated_at for audit. Flash code on success.

Public registration route. New GET/POST /register handler in the public route group (no auth middleware). On every request: short-circuit 404 or redirect to /login if the setting is OFF, so the route does not advertise itself when disabled. CSRF via the existing pre-session double-submit cookie pattern (same shape as login). Rate limit at the middleware layer when CP7 rate limiting lands -- flag this as an integration point.

Patron creation path. Reuse the CreatePatron transactional flow (patrons + users) but with the username chosen by the user rather than auto-generated by generateBaseUsername. Add a username uniqueness check before commit (the existing collision-retry loop is for auto-generation; user-chosen usernames need explicit validation). Password rules: minimum length, bcrypt cost matches existing. No email verification in v1 -- add as a follow-up if abuse becomes real. No admin approval queue in v1 -- the toggle itself is the gate.

Auto-login on success or redirect to login? Probably auto-login + flash welcome -- matches public expectations and avoids a confusing extra step. Decide at design time.

Sidebar and login page. When the setting is ON, add a Create an account link below the Sign In button on the login page (mirror placement of the kiosk link added in cs408-go-stack-qqx). Conditionally rendered server-side based on the setting.

Audit and rollback. Include the toggle event in any future audit log work. Flipping the setting OFF does not delete already-self-registered accounts -- they become indistinguishable from staff-created patrons.

Testing. Unit tests for the setting read/write path. Handler tests for /register in both states (OFF returns 404, ON happy path, ON with duplicate username, ON with weak password). Boundary test: confirm /register is not reachable when OFF even with CSRF and form data set up correctly.

title: [Backlog] Patron address field for printed notices
description: patrons.address TEXT nullable column to support printed overdue notices. Three design options (always-on / skip-mail-flow / optional-with-graceful-fallback) documented in docs/cp6-planning.md. Only relevant when the overdue notice print system is built; track together. Schema change requires a fresh DB since CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS does not migrate. Cut from CP6 v2 trim.

title: [Backlog] Overdue notice print system
description: /reports/overdue table with per-loan row granularity, click-through to per-patron printable notice, browser @media print + @page CSS (no server-side PDF). CP6 ships the 'know what's overdue' half via the /loans?filter=overdue filter; the formatted mailable notice is the deferred polish. Full design including SQL queries in docs/cp6-planning.md. Pairs with the patron address field item -- printed notices need an address. Cut from CP6 v2 trim.

title: [Backlog] Fuller dashboard redesign (mini-lists, richer per-role cards)
description: CP6 ships the trimmed essential version (three staff/admin cards + one patron card, count-plus-target shape). What remains deferred is the richer four-cards-per-role design from docs/cp6-planning.md: patron mini-lists (titles + due dates with 'Due in 2 days' highlighting), Today's Activity card ('N checkouts, M returns today'), My Holds placeholder card (pending holds feature), Favorites card (pending favorites ship), Out of Stock card for patrons (tied to holds). Design principle: every card answers 'is anything urgent', 'what's happening now', or 'where do I go to act'. Subsumes the patron-facing dashboard richer view item.

title: [Backlog] Sidebar restructure (section headers by domain, not access tier)
description: Current sidebar has 'Admin' and 'Staff' as confused peers. Proposed fix: section headers describe domain-of-work (Navigation / Management / Administration, or four-section variant adding Circulation), with role gating applied per-item. Three variants documented in docs/cp6-planning.md. The /admin route is retained but relabeled to 'Data Tools' when the restructure lands. Cut from CP6 v2 trim.

title: [Backlog] Checkout/checkin rapid-scan portal
description: Dedicated /checkout and /checkin staff pages, patron-selected-once + ISBN-rapid-entry flow, scanner-compatible (barcode scanners emit keyboard + Enter). Earns its place at volume; a 10-50 transactions/day library works fine on the per-book book-detail flow shipped in CP6. Variant B design preserved in docs/cp6-planning.md. Cut from CP6 v2 trim on 2026-04-19.

title: [Backlog] Offline detection for the Open Library Lookup button
description:
When the user's browser can't reach OL, hide or disable the Lookup button on the New/Edit book form rather than leaving it to always-fails-until-timeout. Today the button is unconditionally visible and clicking it when offline yields a slow timeout error rather than a clear up-front signal.

Plan is A + D combined:

A. navigator.onLine + online/offline event listeners. Handles the unplugged-laptop and airplane-mode cases synchronously and for free. Listen on window.online and window.offline, hide/show the button accordingly. Initial state set from navigator.onLine on page load.

D. On the first OL fetch failure within a session, set a session-scoped flag (sessionStorage) that hides the Lookup button for subsequent form opens in the same session and surfaces 'Open Library unreachable. Fill in manually.' inline next to where the button used to be. Flag persists for the browser session, gets cleared on tab close so the next session retries OL fresh.

All in app.js. No server changes. No probe endpoint.

Known gap: a network that has general internet but blocks openlibrary.org specifically (corporate firewall, DNS block) still shows the button on first attempt -- acceptable because D catches it on click with a clear message and hides the button for the rest of the session.

If we later want stronger 'actually reachable from EC2' detection (e.g. corporate networks where the server has internet but OL is blocked downstream), layer on a server-side probe at startup or cached per-N-minutes that renders the form with an OpenLibraryReachable boolean flag in the template data. Out of scope for v1.

From #20.

title: [Backlog] Orphan cover cleanup on post-cover-save validation failure
description: HandleBookCreate and HandleBookUpdate save the cover to disk BEFORE the duplicate-ISBN pre-check and DB write. If a cover-save succeeds but a later step fails (duplicate ISBN, transient DB error), the file is orphaned under data/covers/. Low-frequency leak in practice since duplicates are caught client-side before submit in the common case. Two fix paths: (a) stage the cover bytes in memory until the DB write succeeds, or (b) janitor pass that sweeps data/covers/ for files not referenced in books.cover_filename. Same issue exists in Create flow shipped in #20 and Update cover-replacement flow.

title: [Backlog] Password-reset Variant 2 (server-generated temp + force-change)
description: Stronger posture where the admin never learns the user's long-term password. Requires: must_change_password column on users, login-middleware branch that redirects flagged users to a /change-password page, self-service password-change handler + template, one-shot display of the generated temp password to the admin. Variant 1 (admin-chosen password, current implementation) is acceptable for a small trusted staff; Variant 2 is the right long-term default. Applies to both staff and patron reset flows. From #39.

title: [Backlog] Staff/Patron/Book table responsive polish
description: Several issues on narrow viewports: (1) 'Reset Password' button label wraps mid-word; (2) raw ISO-8601 timestamps (2026-04-18T04:54:01Z) break awkwardly; (3) Actions column buttons get cramped below md breakpoint. Options: friendly date format (server-side or via template helper), icon-only action buttons with tooltips, stacked-card layout below md. Same treatment will eventually be needed for patron and book tables, so solve it once and reuse the helper or component. Raised during #39 smoke testing.

title: [Backlog] Favorites (heart books for later)
description: patron_favorites(patron_id, book_id, created_at) table with a composite primary key. Toggle handler at POST /books/:id/favorite (staff+patron route group). Heart icon on kiosk book cards and catalog cards wired to the toggle via fetch() + optimistic UI. GetPatronFavorites DB method for the patron dashboard's 'My Favorites' card. Lightweight feature once priorities allow. Originally CP6 session 6 'if time permits', cut preemptively on 2026-04-24 to buy a real timeline buffer before the 5/1 deadline.

title: [Backlog] Patron holds on checked-out books
description: When a book is checked out, a patron should be able to place a hold so they're notified when it returns. Schema: holds(id, patron_id, book_id, placed_at, fulfilled_at, cancelled_at). New /my/holds page (mirrors /my/loans pattern, route in patron group). Notification on return: server pushes via SSE (see SSE backlog item) and/or shows a flash banner on next login. Auto-fulfilment when ReturnBook completes -- earliest open hold gets fulfilled. UI on the kiosk + book detail: a 'Place Hold' button when QuantityAvailable==0. Originally part of CP6 #22, deferred post-submission with favorites and SSE.

title: [Backlog] SSE live availability updates
description: Server-Sent Events stream so a kiosk or catalog tab updates a book's availability badge in real time when staff checks the book in/out from another browser. Endpoint: GET /events. Subscribe pattern: clients open the stream and the server emits 'book_availability' events with {book_id, quantity_available} payloads on every successful CheckoutBook / ReturnBook. Single global stream is fine for CP6-scale; per-book channel is over-engineering. Replaces the original CP6 'kiosk live updates' scope which was deferred together with holds. Note rate limiting / connection caps if we ever add hold notifications on top.

title: [Backlog] Patron activate / deactivate (soft suspension)
description: Soft-deactivation flag on the patron record so admins can suspend a patron temporarily without destroying the row. Useful when (1) the patron has active loans blocking delete, or (2) the patron leaves the library for a stretch and may return. Estimated 1.5-2h: add patrons.is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT 1 (requires fresh DB since CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS cannot ALTER), activate/deactivate handler pair (mirror staff reset-password shape), login middleware branch that rejects authentication for users whose linked patron is inactive, session wipe on deactivate (same idiom as UpdateUserPassword per DEC-022), Actions-column button that toggles state, 'inactive' badge on the patron row. Maps cleanly onto the existing flash system (patron_activated, patron_deactivated codes). Raised during #21 smoke test, deferred post-submission.

title: [Backlog] Patron metadata column UI
description: patrons.metadata is a JSON TEXT column per DEC-016, left null by #21's create flow. Future work: free-form notes textarea, OR structured fields (student ID, library card number, address, etc.) for libraries that want to track more than name/email/phone. UI shape probably an extra section on the patron edit modal. Cut from #21 to keep CP5 shippable on 4/24.

title: [Backlog] Patron reset-password handler + modal
description: Mirror the staff reset-password pattern (Variant 1 admin-typed). Today's recovery path is 'admin deletes the patron and recreates with a new password' which is acceptable for a small library but not great UX. Estimated work: one POST handler + one modal + ~3 tests. Variant 2 (server-generated temp + force-change-on-next-login) is the stronger long-term path, tracked in the staff Variant 2 backlog item. Cut from #21 to keep CP5 shippable on 4/24.

title: [Backlog] CSV patron import
description: Bulk-import patrons from CSV. Cut from #21 to keep CP5 shippable on 4/24. When un-deferred: file upload widget, header-row validation against patron schema (name required, email/phone optional, password column generates a temp), per-row error reporting (row 5: invalid email; row 7: duplicate name), partial-success transaction (one tx per row OR all-or-nothing -- decide at design time). Probably warrants its own /admin tool route. Pairs with #23 admin panel ZIP work.

title: Loan history view (staff per-patron, patron self-view, admin audit) -- deferred post-submission
description:
CP6 ships only active and overdue loan views. A complete history (returned loans included) is not surfaced anywhere except the per-book panel on the staff book-detail page.

Three audiences need it:
1. Staff/admin should be able to pull a patron's full history (e.g. 'this patron returns books late often').
2. Patrons should be able to see what they have read in the past, not just what they currently hold.
3. Admins running an audit should be able to filter by date range.

Design questions when un-deferred:

Route shape: /patrons/:id/history for staff vs /my/history for patrons -- separate routes preserve the privacy boundary built in CP6 around /loans vs /my/loans.

Endpoint approach: whether to add a ?include=returned flag on the existing endpoints or build a new pair. Separate routes likely cleaner because the privacy boundary is already enforced at the route group.

Pagination strategy: needed if loan rows grow past the all-in-DOM threshold. Same Path 2 (AJAX fragment swap) approach as the catalog pagination work would apply.

CSV export for the staff view: likely useful for audit purposes, slots into the CP7 admin panel pattern.

DB layer impact:
- GetActiveLoans / GetPatronActiveLoans currently filter on returned_at IS NULL -- preserve.
- New GetLoanHistory(patronID, ...) for staff per-patron view.
- New GetPatronFullHistory(patronID, ...) for /my/history.
- Count query for pagination, sharing the same WHERE clause.

Existing LoanListRow struct is reusable -- add returned_at as an optional/pointer field.

Templates:
Option A: extend loans.html and my_loans.html with a 'Show returned' toggle.
Option B: build a dedicated history page that includes a 'Returned on' column.
Lean toward B for cleaner mental separation between 'what's outstanding' (action-oriented) and 'what happened' (audit-oriented).

Privacy: avoid leaking other-patron history to a patron-role user via the same route-group enforcement pattern as /my/loans (RequirePatron middleware, *user.PatronID always used as the filter).

value: modernc.org/sqlite auto-parses any column declared DATE/DATETIME on Scan and re-emits as RFC3339Nano (e.g. '2026-05-09' becomes '2026-05-09T00:00:00Z'). SQL-side strftime() does NOT defeat this -- the driver looks at column-type metadata, not value content. Fix: declare the column TEXT in createSchema(). SQLite functions like DATE('now'), julianday(), date arithmetic all still work on TEXT values that look like ISO dates, so no SQL changes needed. Hit this on cp6-loans session 4 when the patron dashboard's Next due line surfaced the issue that staff /loans had been silently rendering since session 3.

When showing a whole function in Go tutor mode where only part changes, annotate new/modified lines with inline comments: '// <-- NEW' on added lines, '// <-- CHANGED' on modified lines, '// (removed: ...)' for deletions. Keep markers on the right side so code still pastes cleanly (Tim can strip them after). Use on any whole-function presentation where less than ~30% of the function is new; if almost everything changed, markers become noise.

/admin page is a shared tools dashboard for both admin and staff (via RequireStaff middleware). Template conditionally shows sections: both roles see export/import (ZIP) + system stats; admin-only sees link to /staff management and system-level settings. Use {{if and .User (eq .User.Role "admin")}} for admin-only sections. Ensure admin-only POST endpoints are registered in the admin route group, not the staff group. Relevant for CP7 admin panel work.

When working on LibreShelf, do NOT add new entries to CLAUDE.md backlog index, do NOT add design summaries to CP active-scope lines, and do NOT mirror bd issue content into CLAUDE.md. CLAUDE.md is for standing context only: working agreements, hard rules, stack/commands, gotchas, key references. Active tasks and design notes belong in bd + GitHub + project board + DECISIONS.md. The 38KB->12KB trim earlier in 2026-04-25 was specifically to stop this drift. Test before any CLAUDE.md edit: 'is this a rule or a fact?' Rules go in CLAUDE.md; facts go in bd/DECISIONS.md/git. Permanent infra facts (dev environment changes, HTTPS landing) are the only narrow exception.

CP6 loan system shipped 2026-04-25 (PR #42 merged into main, deployed to EC2 same day). Final shape: loans schema is (id, book_id NOT NULL, patron_id NOT NULL, checked_out_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, due_date TEXT NOT NULL, returned_at DATETIME nullable). due_date is TEXT (not DATE) because the modernc.org/sqlite driver auto-parses DATE columns on Scan and re-emits RFC3339 -- see sqlite-date-driver-roundtrip memory. State derivable from due_date + returned_at, no status column: active = returned_at IS NULL AND due_date >= DATE('now'); overdue = returned_at IS NULL AND due_date < DATE('now'); returned = returned_at IS NOT NULL. CountActiveLoans tightened to exclude overdue so the staff dashboard's Overdue and Active Loans cards count disjoint subsets. Constants DefaultLoanTermDays=14 and MaxActiveLoansPerPatron=5 in db.go. Sentinels: ErrNoCopiesAvailable, ErrLoanAlreadyReturned, ErrPatronHasOverdue, ErrPatronAtLoanLimit. CheckoutBook runs three guards INSIDE the transaction (overdue, limit, copies) to avoid TOCTOU. Privacy boundary for patrons: RequirePatron middleware + dedicated /my/loans route in its own route group, NOT handler-branched on /loans -- so a future code change cannot accidentally leak other patrons' loans. Routes: staff/admin /loans + /loans/:id/return + /books/:id/checkout; patron /my/loans; public /kiosk + /kiosk/books/:id (separate kiosk_layout.html shell, renderKioskTemplate skips User/CSRFToken injection). fine_cents column was dropped from CP6 -- will be re-added (shape TBD) when overdue notice + fines feature is built post-submission. 169 tests passing across full suite.

Claude can use Write/Edit tools directly on the createSchema() function in db.go (SQL schema DDL). Still use tutor mode for Go structs, methods, and handler logic. Why: Tim considers schema DDL different from Go application logic.

Claude can use Write/Edit tools directly on Go test files (main_test.go and *_test.go). Still use tutor mode for handler logic, middleware, query methods. Why: Tim considers test setup and assertions closer to boilerplate than application logic.

When a workflow is documented in the project's own docs (e.g. docs/week6/deployment.md), use those exact commands. Do not add optimizations or 'safer' variants even if technically correct. Why: Tim's documented procedures are known-working across his environments and deploys; silent swaps invite new failure modes and make steps harder to audit. Raise improvements separately after the task is done, not as substitutions. Only deviate if Tim explicitly asks or the documented command has visibly failed.

Claude can use Write/Edit tools directly for repetitive data-entry Go code: seed functions, struct literal lists, flash map entries. Still use tutor mode for handlers, middleware, query methods, and other Go logic. Why: Tim considers boilerplate data entry not logic worth learning from.

Loan history view is a deferred post-submission backlog item raised on 2026-04-25. Three audiences: staff/admin pulls a patron's full history (returned + active), patrons see their own past + current loans, admins filter by date range for audits. Privacy boundary must mirror /loans vs /my/loans -- separate routes (/patrons/:id/history for staff, /my/history for patrons) so RequirePatron / RequireStaff middleware enforces it at route-group level, not handler-branched. DB layer: existing GetActiveLoans / GetPatronActiveLoans filter on returned_at IS NULL; new GetPatronLoanHistory(patronID, ...) and GetAllLoanHistory(...) needed plus a count query for pagination. LoanListRow reusable -- add returned_at as optional. Templates can either extend loans.html / my_loans.html with a Show-Returned toggle OR be a dedicated history page with a Returned-on column. CSV export likely useful for the staff view; slots into CP7 admin panel work. Pagination strategy depends on whether the app stays all-in-DOM at the new row volume; pairs naturally with the deferred Path-2 fragment-swap pagination.

Patrons CANNOT self-checkout or self-return in LibreShelf. All checkout/return transactions are staff-only (admin or staff role). Confirmed 2026-04-10 during CP4 post-deploy review, overriding older permission matrix that incorrectly listed 'Yes (self)' for patron checkout. Patrons log in for: browsing catalog, favoriting books, requesting holds on checked-out titles. Role-based template conditionals for checkout must gate on (admin or staff), never include patron. Checkout/return handlers live in staff route group. Do not suggest adding patron self-checkout as a future feature -- intentionally out of scope.

Session close-out workflow on this project: (1) go build ./... and go test ./... must pass, (2) bd close <issue> --reason='...', (3) bd dolt pull (no-op if no remote configured), (4) git add specific files (avoid 'git add .' so accidental files don't sneak in), (5) git commit. NO git push -- this is an ephemeral feature branch with no upstream remote per the session-start hook. Pre-commit hook auto-stages .beads/issues.jsonl. Merge to main locally only when the feature branch is fully ready. The 'MUST git push' rules in the auto-managed beads integration block are overridden by AGENTS.md project-specific section.

Tim is a CS408 student at Boise State University building LibreShelf. Go tutor mode: Tim writes all Go code; Claude shows and explains. Exceptions (Claude may Write/Edit directly): SQL schema in createSchema(), repetitive data entry (seed data, struct literals, flash map entries), and test files. HTML/CSS/JS: Claude edits directly. School coding (Java, C, Python): tutor mode. Work coding (Snowflake SQL, Apps Script, Streamlit): generate and explain fully. Dev environments: Ubuntu 24.04 laptop, Fedora Kinoite desktop with distrobox Ubuntu 24.04 container.

Single-session-per-user enforcement is planned but not yet implemented (tracked for CP8). Currently allowing concurrent sessions for testing convenience. When implementing: in HandleLoginPost, add dm.db.Exec("DELETE FROM sessions WHERE user_id = ?", user.ID) before CreateSession to kill any existing sessions for that user.

Plans are documentation only. Tim does all coding. When creating implementation plans, document them (in conversation or memory), do not implement or use ExitPlanMode. Why: Tim is learning Go and wants hands-on practice. How to apply: create plans as memory/discussion, go step by step, explain code, let Tim write it.

In Go tutor mode, show Tim the full function body (opening brace to closing brace) rather than snippets like 'replace lines X-Y with' or 'add this block above that'. Why: splicing fragments forces Tim to reassemble mentally and he tends to introduce syntax errors at seams (unclosed braces, wrong paste position). Exception: surgical single-line edits where position is unambiguous are OK as fragments. For any change touching multiple lines or restructuring control flow, show the whole function.

When Tim says 'check my work' after writing Go code in tutor mode, read the file and do a code review. Do NOT run go test or go vet in the same turn. Why: Tim wants the review before test output -- running tests preempts the conversation about what's right and wrong in the code. How to apply: read the file(s) and respond with a review. Only run tests when Tim explicitly asks ('run the tests', 'does it pass') or in a separate turn after the review.

CP5 (Staff Mgmt + Book CRUD + Patron CRUD) shipped 2026-04-18 (6 days early on the 4/24 target). Four issues closed: #35 fix test router middleware (b9ce9ed; setupTestRouter mirrors main.go route groups, loginAs and logoutHelper helpers, 3 regression-pin tests for middleware chain); #39 staff management (handlers_staff.go list/create/edit/delete/reset-password with IDOR/self-demote/self-delete/last-admin guards; flash.go cookie-based PRG with HttpOnly+SameSite=Strict, error codes mapped to banner text server-side; UpdateUserPassword atomically wipes target sessions per DEC-022; Bootstrap inline live validation across 3 modals; 20 handler tests + admin-group route boundary coverage); #20 book CRUD + Open Library API (handlers_books.go Create/Edit/Update/Delete + /api/openlibrary/isbn/:isbn proxy; openlibrary.go server-side OL client per DEC-008; covers.go validates uploads by size/extension/magic-byte MIME, stores under DATA_DIR/covers/; cover replacement on edit deletes old file after DB write succeeds; ErrBookHasLoans sentinel blocks delete with form banner not 500; flash_detail companion cookie for 'Added: Title' message; shared book_form.html with Variant B two-button submit per DEC-023; 28 handler tests + boundary coverage); #21 patron CRUD (handlers_patrons.go list/create/edit/delete; CreatePatron transactional with generateBaseUsername first-initial+last-word lowercased non-alphanumerics-stripped + collision-retry loop suffixing 2/3/... using SELECT COUNT FROM users WHERE username = ? COLLATE NOCASE; DeletePatron transactional with ErrPatronHasLoans sentinel; admin-typed temp password Variant 1; username not editable once assigned; 13 handler tests + 12 generateBaseUsername subtests). Catalog polish landed alongside #21: 4-wide grid desktop, letterboxed covers against near-black slot with aspect preserved. CSV import + patron reset-password modal + patron metadata UI deferred post-submission to keep CP5 shippable. 93 tests passing on cp5-crud branch.

LibreShelf's real-world context: designed with correctional facility libraries in mind. Staff accounts represent workers with limited trust -- day-to-day operations (checkouts, returns, catalog) but not system-level access (user management, import/export, admin panel). This drives the admin/staff/patron role distinction but MUST NEVER appear in project documentation or code comments. Frame everything in generic 'small library' terms.

Feature branches always go through a GitHub pull request, never merged directly to main locally. Tim manually reviews and tests the PR before merging. Workflow per session close: (1) push the feature branch to origin, (2) gh pr create with a detailed body, (3) hand off to Tim. Don't run 'git checkout main && git merge' from the assistant side. The CLAUDE.md note that cp6-loans is an 'ephemeral branch (no upstream)' was per-session shorthand at branch creation time, not a directive to skip PRs -- branches still get pushed when the work is ready for review.

Output formatting: no em dashes (use double hyphens), no co-author notes in commits/code/docs (omit Claude Code's default Co-Authored-By line), direct and honest assessments over validation. Why: Tim's explicit preferences. How to apply: check all text output and commit messages before finalizing.

AGENTS.md Testing Requirements rule: beads issues that modify Go handlers / DB methods / middleware / business logic require test coverage in acceptance criteria before close. Test-exempt: docs (DECISIONS.md, CLAUDE.md, docs/, README.md), templates / CSS / JS, schema in createSchema (exercised by handler tests), seed data, flash map entries. If Go code lands without tests and isn't exempt: reopen the issue or create a follow-up task. Project precedent: cs408-go-stack-x25 was a follow-up issue for DB tests after session 2 closed without them; session 3b included tests in the same close-out (cs408-go-stack-8k5).
